title: Tanium Interact Saved Question Drift
spl:
index=endpoint sourcetype="tanium:saved"
| stats latest(mod_time) as m by question_name
| where now()-m > 15552000
cimSpl: | tstats summariesonly=t count from datamodel=Endpoint.Processes by Processes.process_name, Processes.user, Processes.dest | sort - count
description: Saved question drift avoids acting on outdated inventory logic.
value: Saved question drift avoids acting on outdated inventory logic.
grandmaExplanation: Saved question drift avoids acting on outdated inventory logic ‚Äî so we know when protection is off, out of date, or bypassed.
implementation: Find saved questions not updated in 180 days. Retire or refresh for accuracy.
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
‚Ä¢ Install and configure the required add-on or app: `Splunk_TA_tanium`.
‚Ä¢ Ensure the following data sources are available: `sourcetype=tanium:saved`.
‚Ä¢ For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 ‚Äî Configure data collection
Find saved questions not updated in 180 days. Retire or refresh for accuracy.

Step 2 ‚Äî Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=endpoint sourcetype="tanium:saved"
| stats latest(mod_time) as m by question_name
| where now()-m > 15552000
```

Understanding this SPL

**Tanium Interact Saved Question Drift** ‚Äî Saved question drift avoids acting on outdated inventory logic.

Documented **Data sources**: `sourcetype=tanium:saved`. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): `Splunk_TA_tanium`. The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed‚Äîrename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: endpoint; **sourcetype**: tanium:saved. That sourcetype matches what this use case lists under Data sources.

**Detection type** for this use case: Hunting ‚Äî interpret thresholds and fields in that context.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

‚Ä¢ Scopes the data: index=endpoint, sourcetype="tanium:saved". Cross-check against **Data sources** above so indexes and sourcetypes match your ingestion.
‚Ä¢ `stats` rolls up events into metrics; results are split **by question_name** so each row reflects one combination of those dimensions (useful for per-host, per-user, or per-entity comparisons for this use case).
‚Ä¢ Filters the current rows with `where now()-m > 15552000` ‚Äî typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.

Optional CIM / accelerated variant (same use case, normalized fields via Common Information Model):

```spl
| tstats summariesonly=t count from datamodel=Endpoint.Processes by Processes.process_name, Processes.user, Processes.dest | sort - count
```

Understanding this CIM / accelerated SPL

**Tanium Interact Saved Question Drift** ‚Äî Saved question drift avoids acting on outdated inventory logic.

Documented **Data sources**: `sourcetype=tanium:saved`. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): `Splunk_TA_tanium`. The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed‚Äîrename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

This **CIM or accelerated** block uses normalized field names and/or `tstats` over data models. Enable **acceleration** on the referenced models (and correct CIM knowledge objects) or the search may return nothing.

**Detection type** for this use case: Hunting ‚Äî interpret thresholds and fields in that context.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

‚Ä¢ Uses `tstats` against accelerated summaries for data model `Endpoint.Processes` ‚Äî enable acceleration for that model.
‚Ä¢ Orders rows with `sort` ‚Äî combine with `head`/`tail` for top-N patterns.

Enable Data Model Acceleration (and metric indexes for `mstats`) for the models or datasets referenced above; otherwise `tstats`/`mstats` may return no results from summaries.


Step 3 ‚Äî Validate
Confirm that events are present in the index and that the search returns expected results. Compare with known good/bad scenarios if applicable. Verify field extractions and index permissions.

Step 4 ‚Äî Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Table (stale questions), Bar chart.
knownFalsePositives: Broad scope: expect noise. Triage using asset criticality, user role and recent change-management activity; promote to a detection only after stable tuning.
